steps.title: Solve the constants directly
steps.content: Because the equation has infinitely many solutions, both sides must simplify to the same linear expression. Match the $x$-coefficient first, then match the constant term.

steps.title: Check the coefficient condition
steps.content: Simplify the left side: $$\frac{15x+35}{5}-\frac{s}{11}=3x+7-\frac{s}{11}.$$ The right side is $rx-7r$. For the two sides to be identical, the $x$-coefficients must match, so $r=3$.

steps.title: Use the constant condition
steps.content: Now match constants using $r=3$: $$7-\frac{s}{11}=-7(3)=-21$$ $$-\frac{s}{11}=-28$$ $$s=308$$ With $s=308$, the left side becomes $3x+7-28=3x-21$, and the right side is $3(x-7)=3x-21$. The answer is 308.
